data.title: Down More Than 50%: Analysts Say Now Is the Time to Bottom Fish These 2 Stocks
data.description:
The first big challenge in building a profitable stock portfolio? Picking the right stocks, naturally. Whether it’s blue-chip giants known for steady gains, high-risk plays that offer outsized returns, or dividend stocks that let you sit back and collect, every strategy has its champions. But today, we’re zeroing in on bargain stocks.

Everyone likes a bargain. It’s the truth behind the old market cliché of “buy low and sell high.” Investors can build a solid portfolio by bottom fishing – that is, looking for stocks that have taken a hit but still hold the potential for a strong comeback.

With that in mind, we turned to the TipRanks database to uncover stocks that have tumbled more than 50% in recent months but still carry a coveted “Strong Buy” consensus rating from Wall Street. These are companies with solid fundamentals that analysts believe could regain momentum in the upcoming year – they see big upside ahead. Let’s find out why.

First Solar (FSLR)

The first stock we’ll look at is First Solar, a leading player in the US domestic solar power industry. First Solar has been in the business since 1999 and today bills itself as a “solar technology company and global provider of responsibly produced eco-efficient solar modules,” an important set of technologies in the fight to maintain a sustainable climate policy. First Solar is an American company, making it unique among the largest global solar companies – it is the only US-based solar company and does not conduct its manufacturing activities in China.

First Solar’s other major claim to fame is more important. The company is known for its advanced thin-film photovoltaic (PV) modules, the next generation of solar power technology. Thin-film PV modules offer a lower-carbon alternative to the conventional crystalline silicon (c-Si) PV panels that have long been the industry standard – and they offer that alternative without sacrificing quality or performance.

Turning to financial results, we find that in 4Q24, First Solar delivered a mixed report. The company’s revenue came to $1.51 billion, up more than 30% year-over-year. and beating expectations by $30 million. However, the company’s bottom line came to $3.65 per share, missing the estimates by $1.04.

While the shares got a temporary bump in the wake of the results, the uptick didn’t last long with the shares subsequently retreating again. That has happened quite often over the past year; the stock is down 58% since it peaked last June. A challenging environment in the international market has weighed on profits, and the company faces another headwind in the form of the Trump administration’s energy policy – the new President has made no secret of his preference for fossil fuels over solar power.

Nevertheless, this company gets the support of Mizuho analyst Maheep Mandloi, who is impressed by First Solar’s potential in the long term. Mandloi writes, “Our opinion on the sales outlook post-2026 has materially improved. Based on channel checks, we now see better moat vs cSi competition in the US as other U.S. cell manufacturers opt for the expensive/inefficient PERC cells due to FSLR’s hold on the more efficient TOPCon tech. Separately, the stock has likely underperformed YTD given negative sentiment around 45X manufacturing tax credits surviving Republican administration but, even in our base case assuming 45X expires after 2026 (1-year off-ramp), we see less pain for FSLR anyway offset by tariffs, leading to better negotiating power in 2027.”

Mandloi quantifies this stance with an Outperform (i.e., Buy) rating and a price target of $252 that points toward a one-year upside potential of 97.5%. (To watch Mandloi’s track record, click here)

The 25 recent analyst reviews of FSLR shares show a lopsided split of 22 Buys to 3 Holds, for a Strong Buy consensus rating. The stock is currently selling for $127.63, and its $252.67 average price target is practically the same as Mandloi’s objective. (See FSLR stock forecast)

Denny’s (DENN)

For the second stock on our list, we’ll shift gears and go to Denny’s. The restaurant chain, with its distinctive red & yellow signage, its all-day breakfasts, and its reputation as a watering hole for hungry college students at 3am, has been a staple of the franchise restaurant and coffee shop scene since 1954.

Denny’s operates 1,568 restaurants under two brand names, Denny’s and Keke’s; the Keke’s brand, based in Orlando, was acquired in 2022. Of the company’s full chain, 1,493 of the restaurants are franchised and 75 are company owned. Denny’s has locations across the United States, and has expanded internationally – its largest presence outside the US is in Canada, where there are 86 locations. Denny’s has 15 locations each in Mexico and the Philippines, as well as restaurants in Honduras, Costa Rica, El Salvador, and Guatemala. There are even locations in such far-flung places as New Zealand, Guam, the UAE, and Indonesia. Of these locations, 1,499 are branded as Denny’s.

In October of last year, Denny’s announced that it has plans to expand the Keke’s brand. Over the coming year, the company intends to close approximately 150 Denny’s locations, while opening an equivalent number of Keke’s restaurants – effectively swapping 10% of the Denny’s restaurants for Keke’s. Through this move, Denny’s intends to expand its presence in the ‘morning eatery’ category, and to capitalize on the local popularity of Keke’s.

Like First Solar above, shares in Denny’s are down over the past year. In the last 12 months, the stock has fallen by ~51%. The headwinds are different, however; Denny’s management has cited the LA wildfires as a local headwind, and recent extreme winter weather as a wider-spread headwind in northern states. The most serious obstacle to growth, however, is declining consumer sentiment. Inflation ticked up in both December and January, and the macroeconomic situation has grown more uncertain.

In that environment, Denny’s released its 4Q24 and full-year results on February 12. The company’s quarterly revenues came to $114.7 million, relatively flat year-over-year but $1.34 million less than had been anticipated. Earnings were reported at 14 cents per share, by non-GAAP measures, missing expectations by a penny per share. Full-year revenue of $452.3 million was down 2.5% y/y.

The results hardly helped sentiment, but this bit of genuine Americana has caught the attention of Wedbush analyst Nick Setyan, who is impressed by the potential in the Keke’s brand, as well as by Denny’s great asset as a franchise company – its real estate. He says of the company, “First, we believe Keke’s is meaningfully undervalued and underappreciated, and is poised to contribute to a resumption in DENN’s annual 5-7% EBITDA growth algorithm. Second, we believe drivers of upside vs. current 2025 SSS growth expectations at both Denny’s and Keke’s exist. Third, we expect a step-up in share repurchases in 2025, resulting in annual FCF/share growth of 10%+. Lastly, we view DENN’s ~$100M in owned property and interest rate swaps that ensure an interest rate on its debt in the low-5% range through 2033 as not fully appreciated by investors.”

The 5-star analyst puts an Outperform (i.e., Buy) rating on DENN shares. He complements that with a price target of $8.50, suggesting an 87% gain for the shares in the next 12 months. (To watch Setyan’s track record, click here)

There are 6 recent analyst reviews on file for Denny’s stock and these break down into 5 Buys and 1 Hold, making the consensus view a Strong Buy. The stock is priced at $4.55 and its average price target, $7.42, implies a potential one-year upside of 63%. (See DENN stock forecast)

To find good ideas for stocks trading at attractive valuations, visit TipRanks’ Best Stocks to Buy, a tool that unites all of TipRanks’ equity insights.

Disclaimer: The opinions expressed in this article are solely those of the featured analysts. The content is intended to be used for informational purposes only. It is very important to do your own analysis before making any investment.

data.title: T, CVX, or ET: Which “Strong Buy” Dividend Stock Can Deliver the Highest Returns?
data.description:
Dividend stocks help enhance the overall returns of investors’ portfolios. Their attractiveness increases even more in a low interest rate environment. Using TipRanks’ Stock Comparison Tool, we placed three “Strong Buy” dividend stocks: AT&T (T), Chevron (CVX), and Energy Transfer (ET), against each other to pick the one that could deliver the highest total returns (dividend yield plus stock price appreciation), according to Wall Street analysts.

AT&T (NYSE:T)

Telecom giant AT&T recently reported better-than-expected fourth-quarter results, driven by strong demand for its discounted premium plans that integrate 5G mobile with high-speed fiber data services. The company added 482,000 net monthly bill-paying wireless phone subscribers and 307,000 new fiber customers in Q4 2024.

Looking ahead, AT&T expects capital expenditure in the $22 billion range again this year, which it aims to direct toward modernizing its wireless network and expanding the reach of its fiber network. The company expects to make shareholder returns worth more than $40 billion over the next three years, including more than $20 billion in dividends.

AT&T lost its dividend aristocrat status in 2022, when it spun off its media business and reduced its dividend. However, several investors continue to view AT&T as an attractive dividend stock due to its attractive yield. With a dividend per share of $0.2775, T stock offers a dividend yield of 4.1%.

Is AT&T a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Last month, Tigress Financial analyst Ivan Feinseth increased his price target for AT&T stock to $32 from $30 and maintained a Buy rating. The five-star analyst noted that AT&T continues to benefit from solid wireless and broadband subscriber growth and 5G network expansion. Feinseth believes that the ongoing 5G and fiber network and broadband rollout, along with ongoing cost reductions, will continue to fuel the company’s growth.

With 20 Buys versus four Hold recommendations, AT&T stock earns a Strong Buy consensus rating. The average T stock price target of $27.65 indicates that the stock is fully valued at current levels. AT&T stock has rallied 63% over the past year.

See more T analyst ratings

Chevron (NYSE:CVX)

Oil and gas giant Chevron gained immensely from the post-pandemic surge in oil prices. However, that rally has subsided. The company’s refining business slipped into a loss for the first time since 2020 in Q4 2024. Weak refining margins and lower oil prices dragged down Chevron’s bottom line in the fourth quarter.

The company returned $27 billion to shareholders in 2024, including $15.2 billion in share repurchases and $11.8 billion in dividends. Remarkably, Chevron has returned more than $75 billion to shareholders over the past three years.

Chevron declared a 5% hike in its quarterly dividend per share to $1.71 per share, payable on March 10, 2025. CVX stock offers a dividend yield of 4.3%.

Is CVX a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

Following the Q4 2024 print, Mizuho analyst Nitin Kumar increased the price target for Chevron stock to $203 from $194 and reiterated a Buy rating, calling it a Top Pick. The analyst updated his CVX model to reflect Q4 results and the new 2025-26 outlook. Kumar noted that the headline CFFO (cash flow from operations) miss due to one-time items took some shine off the Q4 results.

However, he remains optimistic as the 2025 to 2026 outlook indicates that company can deliver an incremental $10 billion in free cash flow by 2026 by slowing the pace of investments in organic projects and delivering about $2 to $3 billion of structural cost reductions.

Overall, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Chevron stock based on 12 Buys and three Holds. The average CVX stock price target of $176.47 implies 15.3% upside potential. CVX stock has almost been flat over the past year.

See more CVX analyst ratings

Energy Transfer (NYSE:ET)

Midstream energy company Energy Transfer recently announced its fourth-quarter results. The company missed the Street’s revenue and EPS estimates as the contributions from the recently acquired pipeline and terminal assets were more than offset by lower volumes in the fuel distribution segment.

Nevertheless, Energy Transfer is confident about its growth ahead, bolstered by its diversified asset base and strategic acquisitions. The company expects a capex of $5 billion in 2025. It is worth noting that Energy Transfer is well-positioned to benefit from the growing natural gas demand for powering data centers amid the AI (artificial intelligence) wave.

Coming to shareholder returns, Energy Transfer announced a quarterly distribution per common unit of $0.3250 for Q4 2024, which marked a 3.2% year-over-year increase. ET offers a yield of 6.8%.

Is ET Stock a Good Buy?

Following the Q4 2024 results, Citi analyst Spiro Dounis increased the price target for Energy Transfer stock to $22 from $20 and reiterated a Buy rating. The analyst stated that the higher price target reflects the company’s better-than-anticipated EBITDA guidance and “meaningfully higher” near-term, backed by its $5 billion capex. The analyst contended that while the capex guidance came as a surprise, it’s still not the highest among peers as a percent of EBITDA.

Dounis highlighted that management indicated expectations of mid-teens returns, with cash flowing from most of the projects by 2026. The analyst expects ET to be free cash flow positive by FY26. Overall, Dounis expects the existing growth backlog to provide a multi-year runway for Energy Transfer, enabling it to reach the high end of its 3% to 5% growth range.

With nine Buys and one Hold recommendation, Energy Transfer stock scores a Strong Buy consensus rating. The average ET stock price target of $22.88 implies about 20.2% upside potential. ET stock has risen 27% over the past year.

See more ET analyst ratings

Conclusion

Wall Street is highly bullish on AT&T, Chevron, and Energy Transfer stocks. However, analysts see higher upside potential in ET stock than in T and CVX stocks. Including dividend yield, Energy Transfer stock could offer the highest total returns among the three dividend stocks discussed here. Considering Energy Transfer’s major growth projects, including those focused on addressing the demand for natural gas in powering data centers, the company seems poised to grow at an attractive rate ahead.

Disclosure

data.title: BUD, TAP, or SAM: Which Beer Stock Generates FOMO Among Investors?
data.description:
The beer industry is home to some of the world’s most recognizable brands, including three of its largest: Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD), Molson Coors (TAP), and Boston Beer Company (SAM). Over 60% of Americans say they drink alcohol, so beer is big business. Beer drinkers are famous for having their particular favorites for every occasion, but which beer stock looks most likely to hit the spot with investors right now?

Let’s find out which beer stock offers the most compelling investment case…

Anheuser-Busch InBev SA/NV (NYSE:BUD) | The King of Beers

Anheuser-Busch InBev was formed when Belgium’s InBev acquired U.S.-based Anheuser-Busch in 2008. Today, the company boasts a market cap of $118 billion and has affirmed itself as the largest brewer in the world and by far the largest company in this comparison.   

The company’s portfolio features many popular beers, including Budweiser, Bud Light, Stella Artois, Michelob Ultra, Kona, and Goose Island. It also sells the popular Modelo and Corona brands overseas. The company has also branched out into other types of alcoholic beverages with brands like Cutwater and Nutrl. In 2024, BUD brought in $59.8 billion in sales, up slightly from $59.4 billion in 2023. 

Shares of BUD are inexpensive, trading at just 16.2x consensus forward earnings estimates. This places BUD squarely in value stock territory as it trades at about two-thirds of the S&P 500’s (SPX) valuation, which currently trades for roughly 24x earnings. Since BUD also has about $69 billion in long-term debt, it’s worth looking at the company’s enterprise value to EBITDA (earnings before interest, taxes, debt, and amortization), currently at a reasonable multiple of 9.9.

In addition to being a value stock, BUD is also a dividend stock. However, its dividend yield of 1.08% isn’t much to write home about and is just slightly lower than that of the S&P 500, which currently yields 1.3%. However, the company is also rewarding its shareholders with share repurchases—it has bought back $750 million worth of shares since announcing a $2 billion buyback program in October 2024.

Is Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD) Stock a Good Buy Now?

Wall Street analysts are generally bullish on BUD stock. BUD earns a Strong Buy consensus rating based on three Buys, one Hold, and zero Sell ratings assigned in the past three months. The average analyst BUD stock price target of $68.25 per share implies a 14% upside potential from current levels.

Molson Coors Beverage Company (NYSE:TAP) | Budweiser’s Greatest Challenger

Molson Coors Beverage Company was formed through the 2005 merger of Canada’s Molson and U.S.-based Coors. In 2016, the company acquired Miller Brewing Company for $12 billion, making it the world’s third-largest brewing company. Today, the company has a market cap of $12.4 billion and has household-name brands such as Coors, Molson, Miller, Blue Moon, Carling, and Foster’s.

In 2024, the company registered $11.6 billion in sales, a slight 0.6% decline from the $11.7 billion it had brought in in 2023. Molson Coors management attributes the marginal decline to macro-driven consumer softness in the peak sales months of July and August and the end of its contract brewing operations for Pabst, another beer brand.

Shares of Molson Coors are remarkably cheap, trading at a price-to-earnings multiple of just 9.7x forward earnings estimates. This single-digit valuation means that Molson Coors is significantly more affordable than BUD. Like BUD, TAP has debt but looks cheap, with an EV/EBITDA multiple of 7.3.  

TAP is also a dividend stock with an attractive 3.1% dividend yield, more than double the dividend yield of BUD and the S&P 500. In addition to its dividend, TAP returns capital to shareholders via share buybacks. Since announcing its share repurchase program in the fourth quarter of 2023, the company has spent about $790 million to repurchase 13.4 million shares, reducing its share count by about 6.7%.

Is Molson Coors (TAP) a Good Stock to Buy?

On Wall Street, TAP earns a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on six Buys, nine Holds, and one Sell rating assigned in the past three months. The average analyst TAP price target of $65.07 per share implies a 6.2% upside potential from current levels.

Boston Beer Company (NYSE:SAM) | Small But Valuable Up-and-Comer

As the smallest company in today’s comparison, Boston Beer Company (SAM) has a meager market cap of just $2.7 billion and is therefore dwarfed by its larger peers. However, SAM remains the fourth-largest brewer in the U.S., including brands such as Sam Adams and Dogfish Head. The up-and-comer has expanded beyond beer with other popular alcoholic drinks like Twisted Tea, Angry Orchard, and Truly. The Boston-based company pulled in $2 billion in 2024 sales.

While Boston Beer is a great American success story, its shares have become a bit pricey at this point, trading at 23.8x earnings, in line with the broader market. This seems a bit pricey for a company not exhibiting much revenue growth at this point. It also makes Boston Beer Company significantly more expensive than both BUD and TAP.

However, SAM stock has very little debt as a unique selling point, so its EV/EBITDA multiple of 10.2 places it more in line with BUD, but it is still more expensive than TAP.

My other qualm about Boston Beer Company is that it does not pay dividends, meaning it’s not an ideal option for income investors, putting it at a disadvantage to BUD and TAP. However, it should be noted that, like its larger competitors, Boston Beer Company is buying back shares, repurchasing $293 million in 2024, which is no small feat for a company with a $2.7 billion market cap.

Is Boston Beer Company (SAM) a Good Stock to Buy?

Wall Street analysts have collectively assigned SAM a Hold consensus rating based on one Buy, nine Holds, and one Sell rating assigned in the past three months. The average analyst SAM stock price target of $275.50 per share implies a 13% upside potential from current levels.

Molson Coors Stands Tall as the King of Beers for Investment Purposes

All three companies boast strong beer portfolios enjoyed by millions across the U.S. and worldwide. However, only one can come out on top in this comparison.

While I appreciate Sam Adams and its seasonal varieties, the stock trades at the highest valuation in this group despite minimal revenue growth over the past year. Additionally, it doesn’t pay dividends like its competitors, so I’m ruling it out.

TAP saw only a slight revenue decline in 2024, while BUD’s growth wasn’t awe-inspiring either. TAP, however, is significantly cheaper than BUD and offers a dividend yield nearly twice as high. That makes it the clear winner of this boozy showdown—an attractive choice for both value and income investors.

 Disclosure

data.title: Nvidia and Super Micro Computer: Barclays Gives the Lowdown on These AI Stocks
data.description:
AI has been generating headlines and hype, and for good reason. It’s the once-in-a-generation technology that is in the process of permanently altering the tech landscape.

From an investor’s perspective, the key point is how we quantify the AI boom. In 2023, the AI market was valued at $174.3 billion, and it’s expected to reach $2.09 trillion by 2033, for a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of approximately 30%.

The factors likely to fuel the AI expansion include increasing smartphone use, a favorable government/policy environment, an expanded information and communications segment, and the increased use of automation in everything from the factory floor to the car in your driveway.

With a growing number of players scrambling for a slice of this ever-expanding pie, investors are looking to the experts for guidance. Enter Barclays analysts, who are putting two AI stalwarts – Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) and Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) – under the microscope. Let’s take a closer look at their findings.

Nvidia

The first AI stock we’ll look at is Nvidia, a company that has definitely gained a great deal of notoriety recently – and for the right reasons. To start with, Nvidia’s stock has been rocketing upwards over the past few years, the sharp rise in share price propelling Nvidia right to the top of the Wall Street rankings; the company’s $3.05 trillion market cap makes it the second-largest publicly traded firm on Wall Street, behind only Apple.

Nvidia has reached this heady level of success by becoming the market leader in the semiconductor chip industry. All of our digital applications, from electronic calculators to laptop computers to AI data centers, run on semiconductor chips – and Nvidia is a leading designer and distributor of AI-capable chips, chipsets, and accelerators. The company took an early lead in the field of high-speed computing when it invented the first GPUs in the late ‘90s – and saw the chips rapidly find applications in fields well outside of their original gaming niche. Today, Nvidia’s chips are in high demand, and are especially sought after by AI developers and data center operators. Nvidia derives the lion’s share of its revenue today from the data center segment, which is closely tied to the AI field. When it comes to AI-capable semiconductor chips, Nvidia commands about 80% of the market.

The company’s success in meeting the growing demand for AI-capable semiconductor chips is reflected in its earnings. In its fiscal 4Q25 report, the most recently released, the company reported total revenue of $39.3 billion, up 12% from the prior quarter, an impressive 78% year-over-year, and beat the forecast by $1.17 billion. The strong beat was driven by the company’s data center revenue, which came in at $35.6 billion, for a 93% year-over-year gain. Both the total revenue and data center revenue were company quarterly records. At the bottom line, Nvidia realized a non-GAAP EPS of $0.89. This figure was up 71% year-over-year, and beat the forecast by 4 cents per share.

Management guided toward fiscal 1Q26 revenue of $43 billion, which would translate to a year-over-year increase of approximately 65%. The problem for Nvidia, however, is that it has set the bar so high, that the outlook – while ahead of expectations – wasn’t as strong as some expected.

Barclays analyst Tom O’Malley is among those a tad disappointed. That said, he is quick to point out Nvidia’s success has set expectations at a high level. He remains upbeat on the stock, and writes, “From a top line perspective numbers came in a bit lighter than what we thought for April, but this was always the biggest quarter of risk in our eyes. Blackwell upside in Jan combined with H200 growth means a big step down in H100 and the company is pointing to a crossover for Blackwell in April… In all, NVDA has earned a very high bar and wasn’t able to deliver on its usual beat/raise cadence, but we think the company is executing smoothly through the product transition and now trades in the low 20x fwd P/E with the biggest risk to this year accounted for.”

O’Malley gives NVDA shares an Overweight (i.e., Buy) rating, with a $175 price target that points toward an upside potential of 40% on the one-year horizon. (To watch O’Malley’s track record, click here)

Nvidia’s stock has picked up 41 recent analyst reviews, with a 38 to 3 split that favors Buy over Hold and gives the stock its Strong Buy consensus rating. The shares are currently priced at $124.92, and the average price target of $178.66 is slightly more bullish than the Barclays view, suggesting a 43% bump in the coming year. (See NVDA stock forecast)

Super Micro Computer

The second stock on our list, Super Micro Computer, is a long-time leader in the design and construction of advanced, high-performance computer solutions – and in recent years, has become a major player in the development of AI-capable hardware. 

The company is a specialist in AI servers and direct liquid cooling (DLC) and operates an extensive global manufacturing footprint, via which it provides the computing infrastructure that supports data center operations and advanced AI applications.

We should note here that Super Micro’s stock has been falling off in recent months, from the peak values it reached in March of last year. The company has faced a U.S. Department of Justice investigation, looking into alleged accounting violations. During that investigation, the company fell out of compliance with its regulatory filings per NASDAQ listing rules. However, in a press release dated February 25, Super Micro announced that it had received a notification letter from NASDAQ recognizing that the company is now back in compliance with the index’s listing rules.

In accordance with that, the company has also announced that it has filed its SEC regulatory paperwork and financial releases for both Q1 and Q2 of fiscal 2025. Looking at the second-quarter results, we find that the company had revenues of $5.68 billion, nearly $28 million better than had been expected, and saw bottom-line earnings of 51 cents per share, two cents over the estimates.

In his coverage of Super Micro Computer, Barclays’ George Wang gives full credence to both the positives and negatives of the company, pointing out its strong position in the high-performance computer hardware market and its restoration of NASDAQ compliance – but at the end, Wang cannot take an unequivocally positive stance. He writes of the company, “While we recognize SMCI’s leadership position in AI server and direct liquid cooling (DLC) as we expect it would be among the first to ship B200 HGX servers in March-Q, we believe that its competitive moat is shrinking and its checkered past could limit the P/E multiples investors are willing to pay for the stock. We therefore remain on the sidelines despite SMCI now being compliant with its filings, which should help with business fundamentals, such as incremental order wins from customers who were previously hesitant. However, we think that SMCI is still subject to future risks from financial controls and ongoing overhang of potential capital raises to fund the working capital needs for Blackwell purchases.”

In line with those comments, Wang rates SMCI stock as Equal Weight (i.e., Hold) although it should be noted that his price target of $59 implies a 12-month share appreciation of 42%. (To watch Wang’s track record, click here)

Turning now to the rest of the Street, where the stock has a Hold consensus rating, based on 9 analyst reviews that include 3 to Buy, 4 to Hold, and 2 to Sell. The shares have a trading price of $41.46 and an average price target of $45.75, together indicating potential for a 10% upside by this time next year. (See SMCI stock forecast)

data.title: SkyWest’s (SKYW) Stock Dip Screams Buying Opportunity
data.description:
SkyWest (SKYW) shares endured a challenging period during the pandemic but have been resurgent since late 2022. In fact, a well-timed investment would have seen shares rise 700% over three years. However, investors may have an interesting entry point after a recent pullback. I’m bullish on SkyWest, noting its industry-topping expected growth, resilient business model, and strong financial position, complementing its attractive valuation metrics.

SkyWest’s Valuation is Among the Best

SkyWest’s valuation metrics paint a compelling picture for investors seeking value in the airline industry. With a forward P/E ratio of 10.9x for 2025, declining to 8.2x by 2027, SkyWest trades at a significant discount to its projected earnings growth. The company’s consensus earnings per share (EPS) growth rates are robust, with 18.5% expected in 2025, followed by 10.3% and 21.1% in the subsequent years.

It’s also particularly attractive given SkyWest’s strong financial position relative to the industry. With a market cap of $4.1 billion and an enterprise value of $6 billion—owing to its net debt position of $1.9 billion—SkyWest maintains a balanced capital structure. The company’s strong cash position of $801 million provides financial flexibility and a buffer against industry volatility. 

As a result, SkyWest’s EV-to-EBITDA ratio of 6.3x (forward) and 6.9x (TTM) stands out favorably against most peers, indicating an attractive valuation relative to its operational earnings. This metric is crucial in the capital-intensive airline industry, as it accounts for differences in capital structures. For context, 6.3x is in line with many of its peers, but it’s expected to grow earnings much faster. 

Overall, SkyWest’s valuation metrics position it as an attractive investment opportunity within the airline sector, offering a compelling mix of value and growth potential. The company’s PEG ratio of 1.25 suggests a reasonable valuation considering its growth prospects. In fact, that’s a 30% discount to the industrial sector average. 

Moreover, SkyWest’s price-to-sales ratio of 1.15x and Price-to-book ratio of 1.69x further underscore its potential undervaluation compared to some larger carriers.

Resilient Business Model Shouldn’t be Underestimated 

As a regional airline, SkyWest operates under capacity purchase agreements (CPAs) with major partners such as United Airlines (UAL), Delta Airlines (DAL), and American Airlines (AAL). This structure mitigates risks associated with economic fluctuations, as the airline receives fixed payments regardless of passenger volume. 

Although SkyWest does not hedge fuel costs, its major airline partners supply the fuel under CPAs, further reducing operational risks. The airline’s recent multiyear agreement with United Airlines to operate additional aircraft underscores its growth potential. Under this multi-year contract, SkyWest will add 40 CRJ550s to its fleet and plans to acquire 11 used CRJ550s and convert 29 CRJ700 aircraft into the CRJ550 configuration. 

With approximately 500 aircraft in its fleet, SkyWest provides essential regional services across the U.S., Canada, and Mexico. With new capacity coming online, it appears well-positioned to benefit from increasing demand for regional air travel, as evidenced by its projected earnings growth. 

SkyWest Extends Impressive Track Record

SkyWest Airlines has demonstrated an impressive track record of consistently beating earnings expectations. Over the past eight quarters, the company has surpassed EPS estimates every single period, showcasing its ability to deliver strong financial performance. The most recent quarter, Q4 2024, was particularly noteworthy, with SkyWest reporting an EPS of $2.34, significantly exceeding the estimate by $0.53. This represents a substantial beat of 29.3%. 

Additionally, the company’s revenue performance has been robust, with year-over-year growth ranging from 16% to 25% in the last four quarters. This consistent outperformance underscores SkyWest’s operational efficiency and ability to capitalize on the recovering air travel market, making it an attractive investment option in the airline sector. Moreover, with this track record and the recent near 20% earnings beat, it’s possible to expect a more significant upside to the earnings forecast. 

Figures for next quarter are set to be published in April this year, with analysts expecting EPS of $2.06. Once again, there are more positive signs. 

Is SkyWest a Good Stock to Buy?

On Wall Street, SKYW stock carries a Strong Buy consensus rating based on three Buys, one Hold, and zero Sell ratings over the past three months. SKYW’s average price target of $124 per share implies approximately 25% upside potential over the next twelve months.

SKYW Stock in Buy-The-Dip Territory

I’m bullish on SkyWest as its industry-leading growth projections, solid financials, and attractive valuation metrics present a compelling opportunity. Despite recent weakness, the airline’s resilient business model, underpinned by capacity purchase agreements, provides stability amid market fluctuations. SkyWest is well-positioned for continued upside with a track record of earnings beats and ongoing fleet expansion. Given its strong earnings potential and discounted valuation, the current dip appears to be a buying opportunity.

data.title: Jefferies Predicts Up to ~500% Jump for These 2 ‘Strong Buy’ Stocks
data.description:
U.S. stocks staged a comeback on Friday, with the S&P 500 surging 1.59% and reversing a sharp sell-off driven by fears that Donald Trump’s tariffs could disrupt the world’s largest economy.

Among the day’s winners, biotech stocks joined the rebound, with the SPDR S&P Biotech ETF (XBI) gaining 1.85%. The sector has struggled to gain momentum over the past year, but Jefferies analyst Michael Yee sees a window of opportunity.

“XBI is trading roughly flat YTD and it has been a mixed bag overall for the sector with some big wins (e.g., AKRO) and big M&A but has also been a tougher tape for many SMID-cap companies with a prevailing higher-for-longer narrative for rates and investor nervousness around RFK and policy uncertainty. Our analysis finds that an increasing number of biotech companies are trading below cash which reflects the mixed tape – but we believe leads to opportunities for many companies with important catalysts coming this year,” Yee opined.

And these opportunities could translate into significant gains. Few sectors offer the kind of extreme return potential found in biotech, where triple-digit gains – 300%, 400%, even 500% – are not uncommon. But, as Yee stresses, unlocking biotech’s biggest winners comes down to identifying the right catalysts – clinical trials, regulatory approvals, and strategic launches. When those pieces start falling into place, savvy investors should take notice.

Jefferies’ analysts have done the legwork and pinpointed two biotech stocks with strong upside potential – one of which could skyrocket by nearly 500% in the coming months.

And Jefferies isn’t alone in its bullish call. According to TipRanks, both stocks boast a ‘Strong Buy’ rating from the analyst consensus. Let’s dive in and uncover what’s fueling the excitement.

Alto Neuroscience (ANRO)

We’ll start with Alto Neuroscience, a clinical-stage biotech company focused on developing new drugs for the treatment of central nervous system (CNS) and psychiatric disorders. The company applies the principles of precision medicine to psychiatry, aiming to build a pipeline of drug candidates that target core brain processes and address difficult-to-treat conditions such as major depressive disorder (MDD), bipolar depression, and chronic schizophrenia.

What sets Alto apart is its deep understanding of a major challenge in psychiatry: medications don’t work the same way for everyone, and mental health conditions often evolve faster than available treatments. To tackle this, Alto has developed an AI-driven program that analyzes biomarkers from over a decade of testing data.

Alto’s key drug candidate, ALTO-300, is currently undergoing a Phase 2b clinical trial for the treatment of MDD. Designed as a novel antidepressant, ALTO-300 leverages Alto’s biomarker-based approach to provide faster, more effective relief for patients who haven’t responded well to existing therapies. Alto recently shared interim trial results for ALTO-300, which supported continuing the study with a targeted biomarker population of around 200 patients for final analysis. The company is on track to reveal topline results by mid-2026.

In addition to the MDD study on ALTO-300, several other catalysts are lined up for the coming months. The company is set to release Phase 2a proof-of-concept data on ALTO-203 in the first half of 2025. This drug candidate addresses anhedonia, a symptom of MDD and schizophrenia that reduces the ability to experience pleasure and remains difficult to treat with existing therapies. ALTO-203 specifically targets neural pathways involved in reward processing, and positive results could position it as a key advancement in addressing treatment-resistant symptoms.

Meanwhile, data from a Phase 2 proof-of-concept trial for ALTO-101, a potential treatment for cognitive impairment associated with schizophrenia (CIAS), is expected in the second half of 2025. Unlike standard schizophrenia medications that focus on hallucinations and delusions but offer little help for memory deficits, attention difficulties, and impaired decision-making, ALTO-101 aims to address these overlooked cognitive symptoms.

Last but not least, ALTO-100’s Phase 2b trial is underway, with topline data expected in 2026. Though an earlier MDD study fell short of statistical significance, the drug remains a compelling candidate for bipolar depression. By targeting stress-related brain circuits, ALTO-100 introduces a novel approach that could unlock meaningful benefits for a more targeted patient population.

With a pipeline full of upcoming catalysts and innovative approach to psychiatric drug development, Jefferies analyst Andrew Tsai views Alto’s $2.80 share price as a compelling entry point.

“In 2025, we think the stock has the potential to recover on 3+ Phase II datasets, where (+) data could instill investor confidence that mgmt’s biomarker approach of tailoring treatments to maximize efficacy can be viable in psychiatry… the risk/reward looks favorably skewed,” Tsai opined.

Regarding ALTO-300, in particular, Tsai notes several reasons for optimism: “(1) ALTO-300 is already approved in the EU/Australia for MDD broadly. However, it was not approved in the US as the former sponsor (NVS) was unable to have the same dose succeed in two Phase III studies, which speaks to level of inconsistency that comes with all-comer studies, (2) ALTO-300’s prior open-label 8-week Phase IIa data showed a -17 point MADRS benefit in bio+ vs -12.3 points in bio-, (3) We think ‘300’s safety is manageable at the lower dose of 25mg, (4) Note mgmt prospectively removed N=52 patients from this ‘300 study following site/subject case reviews, which raises the chances the Phase IIb is enrolling actual MDD patients.”

With these factors in play, Tsai rates ANRO a Buy with a $17 price target, implying a robust 507% upside from current levels. (To watch Tsai’s track record, click here)

Wall Street echoes his bullish stance. ANRO holds a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 4 unanimous positive reviews in the past 3 months. With an average price target of $15, the stock could surge 435% higher in the next year. (See ANRO stock forecast)

Instil Bio (TIL)

Next up is Instil Bio, a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company that has undergone a transformation. Initially, the company was centered on developing tumor-infiltrating lymphocyte (TIL) therapies. However, as part of a strategic pivot, Instil has shifted its focus toward in-licensing bispecific antibodies.

This transformation gained momentum in August 2024, when Instil entered into a partnership with Chinese company ImmuneOnco Biopharmaceuticals to develop two drug candidates: SYN2510, a PD-L1xVEGF bispecific antibody, and IMM27M, a next-generation anti-CTLA-4 antibody. Through this collaboration, Instil secured global development and commercialization rights for these assets outside of Greater China.

In mid-January 2025, ImmuneOnco initiated the first dose in a Phase 1b/2 clinical trial of SYN2510, in combination with chemotherapy, for patients with advanced non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) in China. Initial clinical data is expected in the second half of 2025. Meanwhile, Instil plans to begin enrollment in a U.S. trial evaluating SYN-2510 in combination with chemotherapy as a treatment for first-line (1L) NSCLC in 2H25, pending regulatory approval.

In addition to NSCLC, Instil Bio is exploring the potential of SYN2510 for treating triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC). A Phase 1b/2 trial in China is planned to evaluate SYN2510 in combination with chemotherapy for first-line TNBC patients, with the study expected to begin in 1H25.

Among the supporters is Jefferies analyst Kelly Shi, who sees considerable potential in the stock.

“TIL’s initial focus is on NSCLC (Ph2 to start in 2H25) and TNBC with China partner running trials in multiple other indications with dose escalation data update in 1H25. SYN2510 presents a unique molecular design: 1) VEGF arm uses ‘Trap’ (VEGFR fusion protein) vs bev (mAb) used in ivo and BNT327; 2) an intact Fc domain vs silenced in ivo and BNT327; 3) PD-L1 (same w/ BNT327) vs PD-1 in ivo. The early (subtherapeutic doses) dose-esc data showed similar ORR vs BNT327. So far, we don’t see enough evidence for differentiation. However, even the base case scenario (similar clinical profile to SMMT’s or BNTX’s) should reflect a largely undervalued opportunity. As the third asset with global trials in plan, we see significant upside for TIL shares,” Shi stated.

These comments support Shi’s Buy rating on TIL, and her $52 price target suggests a potential one-year upside of 158%. (To watch Shi’s track record, click here)

For the most part, other analysts are on the same page. With 3 Buys and 1 Hold, the word on the Street is that TIL is a Strong Buy. The stock’s $20.10 trading price and $114 average price target together imply an upside of 467% for the coming year. (See TIL stock forecast)

data.title: Why Schwab’s SCHD ETF is a No-Brainer for Risk Conscious Investors
data.description:
It has been a volatile start to 2025 for growth and technology stocks. While many have raced out to juicy gains, we’ve also seen previous investor favorites like Applovin and Palantir nosedive 25%, illustrating the true extent of sentiment volatility pervading the market. Investors are concerned about inflation, the effects of potential tariffs, and elevated valuations ripening for a fall. This backdrop makes it as good a time as any to return to basics and consider investing in some tried-and-true value-oriented dividend stocks. Whether you’re looking to avoid market volatility or add a more defensive holding to your portfolio to complement high-flying but volatile growth stocks, the Schwab U.S. Dividend Equity ETF (SCHD) is worth consideration.

I’m bullish on SCHD based on its diversified and inexpensively valued portfolio, attractive 3.5% dividend yield, and cost-effective expense ratio. Additionally, sell-side analysts collectively rate it as Moderate Buy and view it as having a double-digit percentage upside over the next 12 months.

SCHD ETF has appreciated 13% over the past year, 21% over the past three years, and 95% over five years. Over ten years, the fund’s return stands at 189%. When it comes to consistent performance and reliable returns, this is it.

What is the SCHD’s Market Strategy? 

SCHD tracks the Dow Jones U.S. Dividend 100 Index and is one of the most popular ETFs out there today, with assets under management (AUM) of nearly $70 billion. According to Charles Schwab, SHCD is “focused on the quality and sustainability of dividends” and “invests in stocks selected for fundamental strength relative to their peers, based on financial ratios.”

SCHD holds 100 stocks, and its top 10 holdings account for 42.8% of its assets, so this is a reasonably diversified fund. You can check out an overview of SCHD’s top 10 holdings below, using TipRanks’ holdings tool. 

SCHD invests primarily in blue-chip U.S. dividend stocks spanning an array of industries and sectors. Its top 10 holdings include consumer staples giants like Coca-Cola (KO) and Pepsi (PEP), big-name healthcare and pharma stocks like Abbvie (ABBV), Amgen (AMGN), Pfizer (PFE), and Bristol-Myers Squib (BMY), and dividend-paying tech stocks like Cisco (CSCO) and Texas Instruments (TXN). 

In addition to paying dividends, an advantage to owning these stocks is that they are quite a bit cheaper than the broader market. The S&P 500 trades at nearly 26x trailing 12-month earnings, while SCHD’s portfolio trades for just 17 times earnings (as of the end of the most recent month), meaning that SCHD is about one-third cheaper than the S&P 500. The inexpensive valuation of SCHD’s holdings and its strong dividend yield give SCHD a strong defense against market volatility and pullbacks. 

Unlike some giant tech firms, you’re unlikely to see double-digit daily declines for defensive ETFs like SCHD, making it a neat fit for conservative and more risk-averse investors.

Attractive Combination of Dividend Growth and Yield

As the name implies, SCHD is a strong choice for dividend seekers. The fund currently yields an appealing 3.5% per year which is significantly higher than the S&P 500’s 1.3%. Additionally, the fund demonstrates consistent dividend growth after increasing its dividend payout for 13 consecutive years.

Moreover, the fund features a remarkably favorable expense ratio of just 0.06%. An 0.06% expense ratio means that an investor putting $10,000 into SCHD will pay just $6 in fees on the investment annually. The savings from investing in a cost-effective ETF like SCHD tend to add up over time. Assuming that SCHD maintains this expense ratio and returns 5% per year going forward, the same investor putting $10,000 into SCHD will pay just a mere $34 in fees over the next five years. 

SCHD’s Performance Proves the Value of Diversification

SCHD has put up a solid performance over time. As of January 31, the fund has generated a strong five-year annualized return of 11.9%. Over the past 10 years, the fund has posted a strong 10-year annualized return of 11.6%. 

How does this stack up compared to the S&P 500? Using the Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (VOO) as a benchmark, VOO has turned in an annualized five-year return of 15.11% and an annualized 10-year return of 13.7%. While SCHD has underperformed the broader market, it’s hard to quibble with double-digit annualized performance over five- and 10-year time spans. If we enter a more challenging market environment, SCHD may perform better than the broad-market ETF based on its lower valuation and superior dividend.

Is a SCHD a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

On Wall Street, SCHD earns a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 55 Buys, 43 Holds, and two Sell ratings assigned in the past three months. The average analyst SCHD price target of $31.38 implies an 11.38% upside potential from current levels.

Notably, the stocks held by SCHD with the highest upside potential include Guess (GES), Leggett & Platt (LEG), and Apa Corp (APA). In contrast, the stocks with the highest downside potential are The Hershey Company (HSY), Bank of Hawaii (BOH), Paychex (PAYX), and Altria (MO).

SCHD is a Safe Bet in a Dangerously Risky Market

Putting it all together, SCHD is a solid choice for long-term investors who want to mitigate market volatility and invest in a diversified group of inexpensive, blue-chip dividend stocks. The added bonus is that SCHD’s stock picks are executed in accordance with strong fundamentals, seeking to maximize returns while minimizing risk. If seeking to profit from the ongoing tech renaissance, it may be a good idea to hedge and diversify as much as reasonably possible. I’m bullish on SCHD based on its inexpensive holdings, attractive dividend yield history of dividend growth, and cost-effective expense ratio while maintaining exposure to the hottest sector in the stock market.

data.title: Down 30% (or More): J.P. Morgan Says These 2 Beaten-Down Stocks Are Set to Rebound
data.description:
The past few years have fueled a long-term bull market, yet even in this wave of growth, not all stocks have enjoyed the spoils. It’s true that while a rising tide lifts most boats, a few still get left behind. But that doesn’t mean they’re destined to stay in the doldrums forever. For investors who look closely, a few of these laggards might just hold overlooked potential.

That’s where J.P. Morgan’s analysts step in, spotting stocks that may be on the verge of a turnaround. The bank’s experts have zeroed in on two names that have tumbled at least 30% recently – yet still earn strong endorsements from the banking giant, signaling potential for a rebound.

But what does the broader analyst community think? We turned to the TipRanks database for a closer look. Let’s dive in.

Venture Global (VG)

We’ll start in the world of liquefied natural gas, one of the most important energy sources fueling today’s economy. Natural gas has long been acknowledged as the cleanest-burning of the fossil fuels, and liquefied natural gas, or LNG, is a form of the fuel processed for stability and safety in long-distance transport. Venture Global, the first stock on our list today, operates in Louisiana as a long-term, low-cost supplier of American-produced LNG. The company has four export projects based on Louisiana’s Gulf Coast, where they can facilitate the loading and export of LNG into the world energy network.

This company reaps the benefit of its global business footprint. As the name suggests, Venture Global has offices around the world – in Arlington, Virginia, across the Potomac from Washington; and in Houston, London, Tokyo, and Singapore. All of these are major world trade and business hubs, fit to command the trade in a vital commodity such as LNG. In addition, the Gulf Coast accounts for some 87% of the US offshore natural gas production, and the region is a major hub for LNG processing and export, making it an important portal for the entry of North American natural gas into the world energy market.

We should note here that VG is a new stock on the public market, having held its IPO in January this year. The event saw the stock start trading on January 24, with 70 million shares made available at an opening share price of $25. The IPO was considered somewhat disappointing; the stock closed its first day at $24 and is currently down by 42% from the initial price. Nevertheless, Venture Global did report that it raised $1.75 billion from its IPO.

Jeremy Tonet, one of JPM’s 5-star analysts, sees plenty of reasons for investors to take a bullish stance on Venture Global, starting with the company’s record of success and continuing to the likely outlook for the LNG market. He writes of the stock, “VG’s proven track record and speed-to-market advantages stand out at a time when LNG project execution challenges continue shifting time lines to the right for many competitors. Backed by a bullish thesis on long-term LNG spreads, VG carries significantly higher short-term exposure than peers, creating a unique equity instrument play on LNG, in our view. While acknowledging volatility and longer-term price risk, current spot LNG spreads, multiples above ~$2.00-2.50/mmbtu contract prices, position VG to capture outsized near-term profits. A robust endorsement of the 2025 outlook alongside the inaugural earnings and a CP2 FID this year represent key potential catalysts, with sustained LNG price strength and successful future development presenting sizeable upside potential to our valuation over time.”

Acknowledging the potential headwinds of the LNG market, Tonet goes on to explain why this stock remains a sound opportunity: “Although the prospects for Russian gas reentering the European market have roiled LNG sentiment and present notable curve downside risk, we see the VG pullback as overdone and presenting an attractive entry point.”

Altogether, these comments support Tonet’s Overweight (i.e., Buy) rating on the shares, which he believes will climb back up to $25 in the coming year, suggesting a 79.5% upside potential. (To watch Tonet’s track record, click here)

This stock has picked up 10 analyst reviews in the short time it has been on the public market, and the split – 6 to 4 favoring Buy over Hold – gives the shares a Moderate Buy consensus rating. The stock is currently selling for $13.92, and its $21.40 average target price implies a potential gain of 54% over the next 12 months. (See VG stock forecast)

Magna International (MGA)

Headquartered in Ontario, Canada and founded in 1957, the second stock on our list, Magna International, is an important player in today’s automotive industry. The company is one of the world’s largest providers of original auto parts and is well-known for its expertise in the design, engineering, and manufacturing of original equipment. Today, Magna provides OEMs for a host of international names in the automotive industry, including ones such as Ford and Honda, Stellantis and VW, as well as to specialty automakers such as McLaren and Ferrari, and even to electric car makers like Rivian and Geely.

Magna also provides parts and services for manufacturers of medium and heavy trucks and is an important parts provider for the drivetrain manufacturer Borg-Warner, the agricultural vehicle producer John Deere, Harley Davidson Motorcycles, and even the aircraft manufacturer Bombardier. Put simply, Magna International is a manufacturing company – that makes it possible for other manufacturers to work.

Magna International has a truly international footprint, employing more than 170,000 people at 447 facilities in 28 countries. The company is consistently profitable – although the stock has fallen in recent months, losing 30% over the past year and currently trading just a touch above its 52-week low point.

When we turn to Magna’s financial results, we find that in 4Q24, the last period reported, Magna had quarterly sales of $10.6 billion, up 1.4% year-over-year and described as ‘in-line’ with light vehicle production on the global market. The company reported a non-GAAP EPS of $1.69 and had quarterly cash from operating activities of $1.9 billion. The company’s full-year sales for 2024 came to $42.8 billion, flat from the prior year.

Looking ahead, Magna sees headwinds impacting 2025 sales, but easing up in 2026. Full-year sales for 2025 are predicted to hit the range of $38.6 billion to $40.2 billion, and to rebound back to a range of $40.5 billion to $42.6 billion in 2026. The company is forecasting free cash flow of $1.5 billion or better by next year.

Assessing the company’s prospects, JPM analyst Ryan Brinkman notes several reasons why investors should buy into Magna. Brinkman says, “We see several reasons to believe the guidance may not be as challenging for Magna to achieve than appears at first blush, including: (1) Magna has a recent track record of driving margin improvement independent of (and even in the face of) lower sales as a result of such efficiencies… (2) Our anticipation Magna can improve EV related economics, given that a fair portion of the ‘other initiatives’ helping in 2024 appears to relate to compensation from customers as a result of delayed, cancelled, or lower than expected volume on various EV programs… (3) Management expects to derive savings from insourcing and vertical integration initiatives outside the scope of normal operational efficiency efforts; and (4) Fully $2.0 bn of the expected -$3.4 bn of lower y/y sales at the midpoint of Magna’s 2025 revenue outlook relates to headwinds from foreign currency translation and from the completion of contract manufacturing of JLR vehicles in its Complete Vehicles Assembly business, suggesting less needed contribution from traditional efficiency efforts.”

Summing up his stance, the analyst writes, “Our Overweight rating relative to peers reflects low and below-average valuation… Magna also has strong free cash generation and an under-levered balance sheet, providing opportunities for shareholder-friendly actions.”

Along with the Overweight (i.e., Buy) rating, Brinkman puts a price target of $53 on the shares, pointing toward a one-year upside potential of 43%. (To watch Brinkman’s track record, click here)

Turning now to the rest of the Street, where Magna shares have a Moderate Buy consensus rating, based on 15 recent analyst reviews that include 5 to Buy, 9 to Hold, and 1 to Sell. The shares are priced at $37.11 and their $46.13 average price target indicates that the stock has a 24% upside potential by this time next year. (See MGA stock forecast)
